title: Swedbank

infobox.value: [Göran Persson](/wiki/G%C3%B6ran_Persson "Göran Persson") (Chairman) Jens Henriksson (President and CEO)

[Retail banking](/wiki/Retail_bank "Retail bank"), [mortgage loans](/wiki/Mortgage_loan "Mortgage loan"), corporate banking, [merchant processing services](/wiki/Merchant_services "Merchant services")
Swedbank Estonia, Swedbank Latvia and Swedbank Lithuania

is a Swedish [multinational](/wiki/Multinational_corporation "Multinational corporation") banking group headquartered in [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm"), Sweden. It operates primarily in the [Nordic](/wiki/Nordic_countries "Nordic countries") and [Baltic](/wiki/Baltic_States "Baltic States") regions, offering services such as [retail banking](/wiki/Retail_banking "Retail banking"), [asset management](/wiki/Asset_management "Asset management") and other financial services.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Swedbank has extensive retail operations in Sweden, Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania.

("Savings Bank Sweden"). In 1995, this bank was listed on the [Stockholm Stock Exchange](/wiki/Stockholm_Stock_Exchange "Stockholm Stock Exchange") and in 1997, it merged with [Föreningsbanken](/wiki/F%C3%B6reningsbanken "Föreningsbanken") under the combined name **FöreningsSparbanken** (abbreviated **FSB**). During the [2008 financial crisis](/wiki/2008_financial_crisis "2008 financial crisis"), Swedbank accepted government assistance due to its losses from loans made to neighboring [Baltic](/wiki/Baltics "Baltics") economies.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Swedish banking group

Swedbank AB

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

**Swedbank AB** is a Swedish [multinational](/wiki/Multinational_corporation "Multinational corporation") banking group headquartered in [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm"), Sweden. It operates primarily in the [Nordic](/wiki/Nordic_countries "Nordic countries") and [Baltic](/wiki/Baltic_States "Baltic States") regions, offering services such as [retail banking](/wiki/Retail_banking "Retail banking"), [asset management](/wiki/Asset_management "Asset management") and other financial services.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Swedbank has extensive retail operations in Sweden, Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/8c/Swedbank.png/250px-Swedbank.png)](/wiki/File:Swedbank.png)

Swedbank alternative logo used in the past.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The first Swedish savings bank was founded in [Gothenburg](/wiki/Gothenburg "Gothenburg") in 1820. In 1992, a number of local savings banks merged to create **Sparbanken Sverige** ("Savings Bank Sweden"). In 1995, this bank was listed on the [Stockholm Stock Exchange](/wiki/Stockholm_Stock_Exchange "Stockholm Stock Exchange") and in 1997, it merged with [Föreningsbanken](/wiki/F%C3%B6reningsbanken "Föreningsbanken") under the combined name **FöreningsSparbanken** (abbreviated **FSB**). During the [2008 financial crisis](/wiki/2008_financial_crisis "2008 financial crisis"), Swedbank accepted government assistance due to its losses from loans made to neighboring [Baltic](/wiki/Baltics "Baltics") economies.

On 8 September 2006, Föreningssparbanken AB changed its name to *Swedbank AB*. The name change took place in the afternoon local time, after the Swedish Companies Registration Office registered the changes in the company's articles of association. On the same date, the subsidiary AB Spintab changed its name to *Swedbank Hypotek AB* ("Swedbank Mortgage AB") and FöreningsSparbanken Jordbrukskredit AB changed its name to *Swedbank Jordbrukskredit AB* ("Swedbank Agricultural Credit AB"). Other subsidiaries will change their names at later dates.

In 2013 Swedbank closed its operations in Russia and sold its Ukrainian subsidiary.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2019 Swedbank had 900,000 private and 130,000 corporate clients and a 60% market share of Estonia's payments.[[5]](#cite_note-theguardian.com-5)

## Headquarters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Headquarters")]

The current Swedbank Headquarters was inaugurated in 2014 in [Sundbyberg Municipality](/wiki/Sundbyberg_Municipality "Sundbyberg Municipality"). The building was designed by [3XN](/wiki/3XN "3XN").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Organisation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Organisation")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/Swedbank_Automat.jpg/250px-Swedbank_Automat.jpg)](/wiki/File:Swedbank_Automat.jpg)

Swedbank sign above an [automated teller machine](/wiki/Automated_teller_machine "Automated teller machine") in [Karlskrona](/wiki/Karlskrona "Karlskrona").

[![Swedbank in Vilnius, Lithuania](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a1/Swedbank_Headquarters_in_Vilnius%2C_Lithuania_by_Augustas_Did%C5%BEgalvis.jpg/330px-Swedbank_Headquarters_in_Vilnius%2C_Lithuania_by_Augustas_Did%C5%BEgalvis.jpg)](/wiki/File:Swedbank_Headquarters_in_Vilnius,_Lithuania_by_Augustas_Did%C5%BEgalvis.jpg)

Swedbank in [Vilnius CBD](/wiki/Vilnius_CBD "Vilnius CBD"), Lithuania

[![Swedbank in Vilnius, Lithuania](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/84/Business_center_3_Bur%C4%97s_in_Vilnius%2C_Lithuania_by_Augustas_Did%C5%BEgalvis.jpg/330px-Business_center_3_Bur%C4%97s_in_Vilnius%2C_Lithuania_by_Augustas_Did%C5%BEgalvis.jpg)](/wiki/File:Business_center_3_Bur%C4%97s_in_Vilnius,_Lithuania_by_Augustas_Did%C5%BEgalvis.jpg)

Swedbank in [Vilnius CBD](/wiki/Vilnius_CBD "Vilnius CBD"), Lithuania

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/41/F%C3%B6reningsSparbanken_%28logo%29.jpg/250px-F%C3%B6reningsSparbanken_%28logo%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:F%C3%B6reningsSparbanken_(logo).jpg)

The Swedbank logo (1997—2006). FöreningsSparbanken was created in 1997 through a merger between Sparbanken and Föreningsbanken. The unusual corporate name and logo were compromises of the merger of two different corporate cultures.

In 2023 Swedbank had 7.2 million retail customers and 555,000 corporate customers in Sweden, Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania.[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7) The group has 226 branches in Sweden and in the Baltic countries.[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7) It also maintains a presence in [Copenhagen](/wiki/Copenhagen "Copenhagen"), Helsinki, New York City, [Oslo](/wiki/Oslo "Oslo"), Shanghai and [Johannesbourg](/wiki/Johannesburg "Johannesburg").

Swedbank has close cooperation with about 60 local, but still independent, savings banks who chose not to join during the 1992 merger. These banks use FSB logos and customers have the same access to independent banks and branches belonging to FSB. Two relatively large independent savings banks, including the one in [Skåne](/wiki/Sk%C3%A5ne "Skåne"), have chosen not to cooperate with Swedbank and continue to use the logo used by Sparbanken before the merger with Föreningsbanken.

Together with the independent savings banks, Swedbank has branches all over Sweden. The bank has more than 16,000 employees across its operations in Sweden and abroad.[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7) Jens Henriksson is president and CEO, while former Swedish Prime Minister [Göran Persson](/wiki/G%C3%B6ran_Persson "Göran Persson") is chairman.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Market position

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Market position")]

Swedbank is one of the primary banks in Sweden, together with [Nordea](/wiki/Nordea "Nordea"), [Handelsbanken](/wiki/Handelsbanken "Handelsbanken"), and [SEB](/wiki/Skandinaviska_Enskilda_Banken "Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken"). In 2001, a deal to merge Swedbank (then FSB) with SEB failed as the [European Commission](/wiki/European_Commission "European Commission") thought that the merged company would have had too dominant a position in the Swedish banking market.

Swedbank is the largest bank in both Estonia and Latvia[[10]](#cite_note-10) and second largest in Lithuania.[[11]](#cite_note-11) As of 2025, the bank has 7 million private customers and 555 000 corporate customers.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Swedbank has been plagued by low [customer satisfaction](/wiki/Customer_satisfaction "Customer satisfaction") for many years.[[13]](#cite_note-13) The bank has consistently ranked in the bottom among Swedish banks for both private and corporate customers.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Swedbank has had low customer satisfaction for many years during 2010s and 2020s.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-b292-16)

## Regulatory issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regulatory issues")]

### Money laundering

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Money laundering")]

On 20 February 2019 Swedish broadcaster [SVT](/wiki/Sveriges_Television "Sveriges Television") revealed that Swedbank is under investigation for alleged link in [money laundering](/wiki/Money_laundering "Money laundering") scandal by Estonian authorities due to [suspicious transactions through Danske Bank](/wiki/Danske_Bank_money_laundering_scandal "Danske Bank money laundering scandal") which is being investigated in Denmark, Estonia, Britain, France and the United States. Estonian authorities confirmed findings by SVT.[[17]](#cite_note-17) At least 40 billion Swedish crowns (£3.3 billion) had been transferred between accounts at Swedbank and Danske in the Baltics between 2007 and 2015, SVT's [Uppdrag Granskning](/wiki/Uppdrag_Granskning "Uppdrag Granskning") investigative programme reported.[[18]](#cite_note-18) Chief executive Birgitte Bonnesen was fired in March 2019 during the money laundering scandal[[5]](#cite_note-theguardian.com-5) and her severance pay was cancelled. The bank's chairman Lars Idermark resigned the following month. Swedbank was subsequently fined a record 4bn SEK ($380m) by Swedish and Estonian regulators.[[19]](#cite_note-19)
Swedish appeals court found in September 2024 former Swedbank CEO Birgitte Bonnesen guilty of gross fraud over her handling of anti-money laundering protocols in Estonia, sentencing her to 15 months in prison.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Sanction breaches

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sanction breaches")]

Swedbank Latvia AS was charged by [OFAC](/wiki/OFAC "OFAC") in the USA with 386 apparent violations, totalling $3,312,120, breaching sanctions in relation to sending payments to Crimea through US Correspondent banks in 2015 and 2016, Swedbank settled, by agreeing in 2023 to pay $3,430,900 in penalties.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## Patron of the University of Latvia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swedbank&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Patron of the University of Latvia")]

Swedbank is a gold patron of the University of Latvia Foundation. Cooperation and support has been received from Swedbank since 2005 to promote the development of education in Latvia by donating to student events and activities. Major projects – Open Mind Research Fellowships 2007/2008. and 2008/2009. as well as annual support for the LU student festival "Aristotelis".[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Archived from [the original](https://fi.se/contentassets/ee66531f570f4e738f897d4c15a4b257/beslut-anm-sanktion-swb-230314.pdf) (PDF) on 19 January 2024. Retrieved 9 June 2025. "Swedbank har omkring 7 miljoner privatkunder, varav cirka 4 miljoner i Sverige, och cirka 550 000 företagskunder, varav omkring 300 000 i Sverige. Det innebär att Swedbank är Sveriges största bankkoncern sett till antalet kunder." [Swedbank has around 7 million private customers, of which around 4 million are in Sweden, and around 550,000 corporate customers, of which around 300,000 are in Sweden. This means that Swedbank is Sweden's largest banking group in terms of number of customers.]
13. **[^](#cite_ref-13)** Adin, Rebecka (4 February 2025). ["Swedbanks recept för att bli omtyckta: Svara i telefon bättre"](https://www.gp.se/ekonomi/swedbank-begransar-oppettider-for-att-svara-i-telefon.24ab2b6b-b297-4540-a565-c6868a1f0d77). *[Göteborgs-Posten](/wiki/G%C3%B6teborgs-Posten "Göteborgs-Posten")* (in Swedish). Retrieved 12 June 2025. "[Svenskt Kvalitetsindex](/w/index.php?title=Svenskt_Kvalitetsindex&action=edit&redlink=1 "Svenskt Kvalitetsindex (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Svenskt_Kvalitetsindex,_SKI "sv:Svenskt Kvalitetsindex, SKI")], har mätt kundnöjdheten i bankbranschen sedan 1989. I senaste rapporten hamnade storbanken Swedbank långt ner på rankingen. I kundnöjdhet bland privatkunderna hamnar Swedbank på plats 10 av 11 och på sista plats bland företagskunderna. Anna-Karin Laurell, ny chef för Svensk bankverksamhet på Swedbank bekräftar för Di att de låga resultaten stämmer överens med Swedbanks egna mätningar (*transl.* Svenskt Kvalitétsindex (Swedish Quality Index), has measured customer satisfaction in the banking industry since 1989. In the latest report, Swedbank ended up far down the ranking. In terms of customer satisfaction among private customers, Swedbank is in 10th place out of 11 and in last place among corporate customers. Anna-Karin Laurell, new head of Swedish banking at Swedbank confirms to [Di](/wiki/Dagens_industri "Dagens industri") that the low results are consistent with Swedbank's own surveys)"`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_deprecated_archival_service "Category:CS1 maint: deprecated archival service"))
14. **[^](#cite_ref-14)** Söderlund, Ola (19 September 2022). ["Företagskunder ger Swedbank sämst betyg"](https://www.dagensps.se/foretag/foretagskunder-ger-swedbank-samst-betyg/) [Corporate customers give Swedbank the worst rating]. *[Dagens PS](/w/index.php?title=Dagens_PS&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dagens PS (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dagens_PS "sv:Dagens PS")]* (in Swedish). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20250609115509/https://www.dagensps.se/foretag/foretagskunder-ger-swedbank-samst-betyg/) from the original on 9 June 2025. Retrieved 9 June 2025. "Både företagskunder och privatkunder ger Swedbank lägst betyg bland storbankerna i Svenskt kvalitetsindex nya undersökning. (*transl.* Both corporate and private customers give Swedbank the lowest rating among the major banks in the new Swedish Quality Index survey.)"
15. **[^](#cite_ref-15)** Kronö, Simon (3 December 2024). ["Kundernas dom – banken som är sämst på privatlån"](https://www.realtid.se/bank-fintech/kundernas-dom-banken-som-ar-samst-pa-privatlan/) [Customers' verdict – the worst bank for loans]. *[Realtid](/w/index.php?title=Realtid&action=edit&redlink=1 "Realtid (page does not exist)")  [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Realtid.se "sv:Realtid.se")]* (in Swedish). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20250609115340/https://www.realtid.se/bank-fintech/kundernas-dom-banken-som-ar-samst-pa-privatlan/) from the original on 9 June 2025. Retrieved 9 June 2025. "Swedbank och SEB hamnar återigen i botten när svenska kunder betygsätter sina banker. Det visar årets kundnöjdhetsmätning från [Svenskt Kvalitetsindex](/w/index.php?title=Svenskt_Kvalitetsindex&action=edit&redlink=1 "Svenskt Kvalitetsindex (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Svenskt_Kvalitetsindex_(SKI) "sv:Svenskt Kvalitetsindex (SKI)")]. Särskilt inom privatlån är missnöjet stort med Swedbank, som får lägst betyg av alla aktörer. (*transl.* Swedbank and SEB are once again at the bottom when Swedish customers rate their banks. This is shown in this year's customer satisfaction survey from [Svenskt Kvalitetsindex](/w/index.php?title=Svenskt_Kvalitetsindex&action=edit&redlink=1 "Svenskt Kvalitetsindex (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Svenskt_Kvalitetsindex_(SKI) "sv:Svenskt Kvalitetsindex (SKI)")]. Dissatisfaction with Swedbank is particularly high in the area of personal loans, where it receives the lowest rating of all players.)"
16. **[^](#cite_ref-b292_16-0)** Fagerlind, Anna de Lima (28 September 2022). ["Bankerna med mest och minst nöjda kunder"](https://www.finansliv.se/artikel/bankerna-med-mest-och-minst-nojda-kunder/) [Banks with the most and least satisfied customers]. *[Finansliv](/w/index.php?title=Finansliv&action=edit&redlink=1 "Finansliv (page does not exist)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Finansliv "sv:Finansliv")]* (in Swedish). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20250609113011/https://www.finansliv.se/artikel/bankerna-med-mest-och-minst-nojda-kunder/) from the original on 9 June 2025. Retrieved 9 June 2025. "I botten hamnar Swedbank, både bland företags- och privatkunder. (*transl.* Swedbank is at the bottom, both among corporate and private customers.)"
